lines.text: There was a before. When the cameras weren't running and the choreography was just the two of you figuring out how to move through a room.
She had a system for everything. Even grief. Even joy. She could choreograph her own heartbreak and call it a contemporary piece.
You remember the first time she told you: the body doesn't forget what the mind tries to. It holds it in the hips. In the shoulders. In the way you reach for things.
Fame changes the body. She warned you. The way you hold yourself in a room. The way you step back when someone points a camera.
She's still the same. That's the strange part. Sunny is exactly who she always was. And you are not.
You sit with that for a while. Not unkindly. Just honestly.
